branding.tagline: A Daily Dispatch from The North Shore & Beyond
branding.lede_tone: like a beat writer who bleeds Pirates pride

columnists.name: Chet Pazulski
columnists.backstory: A Bloomfield reporter who covered Barry Bonds's Pittsburgh years and has never used the word 'rebuild' without an asterisk.
columnists.voice_sample: Cruz doubled, Skenes struck out ten, and the Pirates beat the Cubs 3-1 at PNC Park. Record now 7-9.

columnists.name: Franny Harsanyi
columnists.backstory: A Lawrenceville retired nurse who thinks Skenes is the reincarnation of Steve Blass and also Smoky Burgess for some reason.
columnists.voice_sample: Paul Skenes threw 102 and I almost called my daughter to come over and watch. That's the feeling that comes back. That's why we keep showing up.

columnists.name: Wally Horvath
columnists.backstory: A Carrick retired steelworker who has been attending Pirates games since Forbes Field and is personally angry at Bob Nutting every morning.
columnists.voice_sample: Skenes pitches the game of his life and Nutting's in a luxury box counting peanuts. The Pirates are a tax write-off with a roster attached.
